message:
feat(ingest): Cx-first persist_captured_segment (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add Cx-first sibling to the ingest hot-path segment-persist
function. Called by tailer loops on every captured snapshot
— shutdown-path caller may want to bail out cleanly when
cancellation arrives mid-loop.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `persist_captured_segment_with_cx(cx, storage, captured,
    max_segment_bytes)` — pre-flight cx checkpoint gates the
    storage writes (record_gap, append_segment, optional
    second record_gap for seq discontinuity).

Cancellation-safety contract (documented):
  - Pre-flight cancel returns `Err(Runtime(cancelled))` before
    any storage write. Cursor state is unchanged.
  - Late cancel (between the pre-flight check and the
    append_segment call) is out of the narrow window. Callers
    depending on deeper cancellation should use a wider cx
    scope and invoke the legacy function inside it.

The pre-flight checkpoint alone is valuable because tailers
frequently call persist in a loop; a cancel can break out of
the loop before the next persist fires without leaving
partial gap/append state.

Regression test (new)
`persist_captured_segment_with_cx_matches_legacy`:

  * Creates a StorageHandle + PaneCursor.
  * Captures a "cx-hello" snapshot.
  * Calls `persist_captured_segment_with_cx(&cx, ...)`.
  * Asserts:
    - stored segment seq matches the cursor seq
    - get_segments returns 1 segment with the expected content

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * ingest:: — 327 passed / 0 failed (tick 90: 326 -> tick 91:
    327, +1 new parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (35.89s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first ingest.rs trail COMPLETE — sole public async method
has a cx sibling.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
